headlines.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
headlines.description: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

headlines.title: Spat at, threatened and kidnapped: British Jews tell of rising antisemitism
headlines.description: British Jews have described to BBC Panorama how they are experiencing a rise in antisemitism.

headlines.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
headlines.description: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

headlines.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
headlines.description: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

headlines.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
headlines.description: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

headlines.title: Two arrested over Kenton synagogue attack
headlines.description: A bottle containing a type of accelerant was thrown at the synagogue in Harrow, north-west London.

headlines.title: New university free speech complaints system to come into force this year
headlines.description: Universities could face fines of £500,000 or 2% of their income if they are found to have failed to protect free speech.

headlines.title: Health visitors call for limits on 'impossible' 1,000-family caseloads
headlines.description: BBC analysis shows the number of health visitors in England has almost halved in the last 10 years.

headlines.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
headlines.description: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

headlines.title: Lyse Doucet: Most Iranians don't expect this truce to last
headlines.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Tehran as tensions continue over the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
headlines.description: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

headlines.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
headlines.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

headlines.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
headlines.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

headlines.title: Chris Mason: PM facing awkward hours ahead as Mandelson questions remain
headlines.description: Mandelson vetting row shows Westminster continues to have the power to surprise, writes Chris Mason.

headlines.title: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
headlines.description: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

headlines.title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?
headlines.description: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when.

headlines.title: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
headlines.description: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

headlines.title: Frustration, apathy and hope: Birmingham divided as extraordinary election looms
headlines.description: Is the UK's second city about to see the biggest political shake-up in more than a decade?

headlines.title: Why your recycled clothes could end up in this South American desert
headlines.description: Old garments from around the world are being discarded in the South American country.

headlines.title: Keeping open all of King's coastal path will be a challenge, says Natural England
headlines.description: Natural England says it will be challenging to keep King Charles's coastal path fully open in parts of the south.

headlines.title: Watch: Trapped boy dangles from moving bus in Australia
headlines.description: The boy, who was uninjured, was dragged for 350 metres after the back doors trapped his arm and school bag.

headlines.title: At least 25 killed in firecracker factory blast in India
headlines.description: Mostly female workers have been killed in the blast, which occurred in Tamil Nadu state.

headlines.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
headlines.description: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

headlines.title: Row over classical music being 'weaponised' to disperse youths
headlines.description: Musicians are furious that the use of classical music to disperse groups is playing to stereotypes.

headlines.title: Reform pledges to review all asylum claims for past five years if it wins power
headlines.description: The current Labour government has already announced major crackdowns on immigration, including disrupting gangs.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Is The Strait Of Hormuz Actually Open (Or Shut) Right Now?
headlines.description: Plus, the government digs in over the Mandelson vetting row.

headlines.title: Have the Democrats found a new strategy to scare Trump?
headlines.description: Why an oyster fisherman with a controversial past is splitting the Democrats

headlines.title: Puss in Boots, voiced by Antonio Banderas, is on an exciting quest
headlines.description: Puss teams up with a fellow thieving feline and Humpty Dumpty to go on an exciting quest.

headlines.title: 'By chance or choice, they have clicked' - why Man City will be champions
headlines.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy explains why he thinks Manchester City will beat Arsenal to the Premier League title.

headlines.title: Fitzpatrick beats Scheffler in Heritage play-off
headlines.description: England's Matt Fitzpatrick beats world number one Scottie Scheffler in a play-off to win the RBC Heritage and claim his second PGA Tour title in the space of a month.

headlines.title: Arteta must prove he won't be remembered as Arsenal's 'nearly man'
headlines.description: Arsenal and manager Mikel Arteta may still have a season of glory - but must prove they are not 'nearly men' after Manchester City loss, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: Record-breaking Bayern win Bundesliga - now for the Treble
headlines.description: Free-scoring Bayern Munich swept to the German title for the 35th time. Now their sights turn towards completing the Treble.

headlines.title: How 'dropouts' hangout' became snooker's ultimate stage
headlines.description: Sheffield's Crucible Theatre was a regional arts centre with a polarising reputation before snooker came along, by chance, to make it a globally famous venue. What makes it so special?

headlines.title: Raducanu pulls out of Madrid Open
headlines.description: Emma Raducanu's absence from the WTA Tour will be extended by at least two more weeks as she withdraws from the upcoming Madrid Open.
